name: Cassie 100m Guinness World Record Attempt
notes: Cassie ran 100m from standing in 24.73s at Oregon State's Whyte Track, ratified by Guinness as the fastest 100m by a bipedal robot.

name: 1st World Humanoid Robot Games
notes: Inaugural World Humanoid Robot Games. Tiangong Ultra won the 100m (26.87s); Unitree H1 took 1500m gold (6:34.40) and 400m gold, plus wins in 100m obstacle and 4×100m relay. Both performances are in the Bioparity ledger.

name: 2026 Beijing E-Town Humanoid Robot Half Marathon
notes: Honor's Lightning (technical model: Robotics D1, Monkey King Team) won the autonomous category in 50:26 over the 21.0975 km course, numerically beating the human men's half-marathon world record (Kiplimo 57:20, Lisbon, March 2026). Honor's Thunderbolt placed 2nd autonomous at 50:56; Spark placed 3rd at ~53:00. Honor's teleoperated Lightning entry finished 48:19 but is auto-ineligible under Recording Rule 1. Event organized by Beijing E-Town (event-organizer tier), not sanctioned by World Athletics.

name: Inaugural ProRL Combine
notes: First Professional Robotics League event in American history. Humanoid and quadruped robots raced on a 50 m spectator-lined course. The 50 m sprint has no ratified World Athletics record at this distance, so event_types is empty; the ledger tracks this in spirit via the ProRL sanctioning body entry but the course distance falls outside Bioparity's anchored record list.

name: 2nd World Humanoid Robot Games
notes: Second WHRG. Organizers confirmed the 100m race becomes a fully autonomous event for the first time. Competitive category spans athletics, football, gymnastics, weightlifting, martial arts, dance, tug-of-war, and pitch-pot.
